meaning: adj.不令人信服的,无印象的,印象淡薄的
examples: Dewey was an unimpressive, rather dull lecturer.
Its own regulatory record is unimpressive: it was Citigroup's principal regulator.
Reports from India Monday said demand for physical gold was unimpressive as a major festival approaches.
After an unimpressive 5-3 start, the Patriots won their final eight games to finish 13-3.
